name: UptimeRobot API
what_it_does: Uptime monitoring service with REST API for creating website monitors, querying uptime statistics, and managing alert contacts. Known for generous free tier (50 monitors at 5-minute intervals).
use_cases: Creating and managing HTTP/TCP/PING monitors for website availability tracking
Querying uptime percentages and response time averages for SLA dashboards
Managing public status page content via API for automated incident communication
Setting up on-call alert contacts and integrating with Slack/PagerDuty webhooks
Building custom monitoring dashboards using aggregated uptime data
not_for: Check intervals below 5 minutes on free tier (need paid plan for 1-minute checks)
best_when: An agent needs simple, cost-effective uptime monitoring for many endpoints with API access to uptime data and alert management.
avoid_when: You need sub-minute check intervals, browser-based synthetic monitoring, or APM.

auth.notes: API key (read or read-write) from account settings. Key passed as parameter in POST requests (not header — unusual). Read-only and read-write key options available. Rate limited to 10 API calls per minute.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 50 monitors, 5-minute check intervals, 1 status page
pricing.paid_tiers: Solo from $7/month (up to 1-min interval); Team from $18/month; Pro from $47/month
pricing.notes: One of the most generous free tiers in uptime monitoring. Free plan includes API access. Paid plans unlock shorter check intervals, more monitors, and advanced features.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API documented with all parameters listed. API uses non-standard POST-only interface with parameters in body. Error responses include stat and error fields. Monitor status codes documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Monitor creation can create duplicates — check by URL before creating. Monitor edits by ID are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: All API calls use POST method regardless of operation type — non-standard REST pattern
API key goes in request body, not Authorization header
10 calls/minute rate limit is strict — batch operations and cache results
Monitor response time data is aggregated hourly/daily — no real-time time series

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. API key in request body (not header) is non-standard. GDPR compliant with privacy policy. Limited security features — no MFA enforcement for API, no IP allowlisting.
